Python:
A, B = [1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]
for a, b in zip(A, B): print(a, b)
#1 4
#2 5
#3 6
list(zip(A, B)) = [(1, 4), (2, 5), (3, 6)]
dict(zip(A, B)) = {1: 4, 2: 5, 3: 6}
zip(x, y, z)   #Can zip more than two

s:str = ''.join([”Hi” for _ in range(10)])  #ONLY way
#Below is the unacceptable, slow way
s:str = “Hi”
for _ in range(10): s += “Hi”
